Content: A developer who builds end-to-end digital solutions — from the database to the interface — with the kind of precision that makes the solution look obvious in hindsight.
BACHELOR OF SCIENCE IN INFORMATION TECHNOLOGY
Built a working system in 24 hours. It was not pretty. It worked. That was enough.
Voted by peers, which is either a great sign or everyone else declined.
Top of the graduating class. The certificate is framed. The imposter syndrome is not.
Did not win. Did not embarrass the university. Both are achievements worth listing.
Certified by the company that couldn't keep its own frontend from crashing. Still counts.
Proved I understand the cloud. Not because I needed to — because someone once said I couldn't.
Formally assessed by the government. Passed. The bureaucracy was harder than the exam.
Learned the rules of good design. Now I break them with full awareness of the consequences.
